title: FINS3666 2025 T1 Assessment 1 Activity 2 Student Guide V2
body:
Student Guide: Assessment 1, Activity 2

Pricing & Spread Models Weight: 10% Submission Deadline: 9:00pm Friday 28-Mar-2025.

1. Overview This assessment involves an application of financial market knowledge and basic modelling to pricing and spreads of Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs). Using real-world intraday data, you will implement a pricing and spread models as if working in a Market Making business.

2. Instructions for Students

2.1. Preparation: • Review Key Concepts: Weeks 1-4, including: o Statistical analysis and Microstructure metrics o Microstructure and fundamental frameworks used in pricing & spread models • Review Week 5 Lecture • Dataset Access: Download the historical order and trade data from Moodle.

2.2. In-Class Activity: Pricing & Spread Modelling

Step 1: Data Processing & Microstructure Analysis • Clean the dataset to handle missing values, outliers, etc (if applicable) • Calculate standard microstructure metrics (if applicable). Step 2: Pricing Model Implementation • Implement a simple pricing model to determine the intraday Fair Value for an ETF, i.e. a CRICOS Provider Code 00098G

real time or indicative Net Asset Value (iNAV).  Step 3: Spread Model Implementation • Implement simple bid ask spread model for the same corresponding Exchange Traded Fund (ETF).

2.3. Post-Class Submission (Due End of Week 5) Submit the following via Moodle: 1. Cleaned Dataset: Provide the cleaned data used in the analysis 2. Analysis Report (max 500 words): Address the assessment questions concisely 3. Code or Spreadsheet: Submit the code or spreadsheet used for your analysis.

3. Assessment Questions

3.1. Pricing Model: • Summarise the results of your pricing model implementation.

3.2. Spread Model: • Summarise the results of your spread model implementation.

3.3. Critical Reflection: • Strengths & limitations: Discuss how well the models performed compared to real data • Real World Applications: How can each respective model be improved ? Focus on key factors involved in determining ETF prices and spreads.

Assessment Criteria & Feedback Guidance

Criteria HD (100-85) D (84-75) C (74-65) P (64-50) F (49-0) Improvement Tips Data Comprehensive, Strong, minor Basic but Minimal No microstructure Clear data processing, Processing & well-justified omissions in incomplete microstructure analysis or poorly calculate metrics Microstructure analysis; all microstructure microstructure analysis; unclear executed steps. efficiently for use.
